agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Nonprofits with higher total contributions show higher winsorized fundraising efficiency due to economies of scale in operational activities.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Thornton (2006) outlines return to scale efficiencies in fundraising.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant but practically negligible positive correlation was found. Nonprofits with larger contributions have a very slight positive association with winsorized efficiency, but the effect size is close to zero.
